executiveSummary: All 4 user stories and all 5 functional requirements from iteration 000001 are satisfied. The BasicTable component is fully implemented following the pretext-tables conventions: prepare() calls are isolated in measure.ts, the component uses useMemo keyed on stable dependencies, no DOM measurement APIs are used anywhere in production code, font constants are properly defined in shared/fonts.ts, and the component correctly waits for document.fonts.ready before measuring. All 19 tests pass, tsc --noEmit is clean, and eslint reports zero errors. One minor observation was found: the CSS file inlines a font string instead of referencing the shared constant.

verificationByFR.notes: BasicTable accepts rows: { id: string; cells: string[] }[] and columnWidths: number[] as required props, verified in src/tables/basic-table/index.tsx.
All prepareWithSegments() and layout() calls reside exclusively in src/tables/basic-table/measure.ts. Confirmed by grep across src/tables/.

BODY_FONT, HEADER_FONT, and MONO_FONT are correctly defined in src/shared/fonts.ts and imported in measure.ts. However, basic-table.css line 3 inlines 'font: 14px Inter, sans-serif' directly instead of referencing the shared constant. CSS cannot import TS constants directly, but the duplication risks drift between the rendered font and the measured font.

The component uses a useEffect + useState(fontsReady) pattern that waits for document.fonts.ready before setting fontsReady=true. useMemo is keyed on fontsReady so measurement only runs after fonts are loaded.
Only @chenglou/pretext is used for text measurement. No getBoundingClientRect, offsetHeight, offsetWidth, clientHeight, or clientWidth calls exist in any production file under src/tables/.

verificationByUS.notes: BasicTable is exported from src/tables/index.ts. Component accepts correct prop types. Renders without errors. tsc --noEmit and eslint both pass.

All prepareWithSegments() calls are in measure.ts only. useMemo is keyed on [rows, columnWidths, fontsReady]. No DOM measurement APIs in table production code. 12 dedicated tests verify these properties. tsc and lint pass.

Cell heights are driven by pretext line counts (N lines → N * LINE_HEIGHT px). CSS uses overflow-wrap: break-word, white-space: normal, word-break: break-word, and box-sizing: border-box to prevent horizontal overflow. 7 wrap tests verify column-width boundaries and height scaling. tsc and lint pass.

Demo page renders BasicTable with 12 rows and 3 columns (Name 200px, Role Summary 300px, Department 220px). Role Summary column contains multi-sentence descriptions that trigger wrapping. Vite config and index.html entry point are in place. tsc and lint pass.

minorObservations: basic-table.css inlines 'font: 14px Inter, sans-serif' (line 3), duplicating the BODY_FONT constant from src/shared/fonts.ts. If the font is ever changed in fonts.ts, the CSS must be updated manually to stay in sync. Consider a CSS custom property or a build-time injection to keep them aligned.
The BasicTable component has no <thead> / header row — column labels are absent in the rendered table. This is not required by the PRD but may be confusing in the demo.
The demo page uses hardcoded column widths (COLUMN_WIDTHS array) without any prop to control them from the outside; this is fine for a demo but worth noting for future extensibility.
wrap.test.ts is in src/tests/ rather than co-located with the component under src/tables/basic-table/. Consider establishing a consistent test file placement convention.
conclusionsAndRecommendations: The implementation is solid and fully compliant with the PRD. The only actionable item is FR-3 partial compliance: the font string in basic-table.css should be kept in sync with BODY_FONT in fonts.ts. A simple recommendation is to use a CSS custom property (--body-font) injected at the root level by the component or a build step, so the measurement font and the render font are guaranteed to match. The remaining observations are cosmetic or stylistic and can be addressed in a future iteration or left as technical debt.
